content.declaration: I declare that I have developed my semestral work independently. All the sources I used are listed in the section References.
content.title: Car Insurance Company

description.title: Description
description.para: The Car Insurance Company Database project is meticulously designed to streamline and manage the critical data integral to car insurance operations. Include some

Key tables in the database such as Customers, Policies, Vehicles, Claims, Agents, Payments, and Branches. The Customers table contains personal and contact details for policyholders, linked to multiple policies they may hold. The Policies table tracks each policy’s details, including coverage type, premium, start and end dates, and associated vehicles. Vehicles are linked to policies, detailing each car's make, model, and Vehicle Identification Number (VIN), ensuring clear identification for underwriting and claims processes.

The Claims table logs all claims submitted by customers, including claim status, amount, and inspection details, facilitating effective tracking from claim submission through to resolution. Additionally, Agents are assigned to customers and assist them in navigating their policy and claim processes, with details stored in the Agents table. The Payments table records policyholder payments, helping ensure smooth billing operations. Furthermore, the Branches table tracks the company's locations, enabling regional oversight and operational efficiency.

This database supports robust querying and reporting capabilities, empowering customer service, underwriting, claims adjustment, and management functions. With secure data organization and retrieval, the system enhances customer experience and operational performance, enabling managers to make informed decisions, minimize risks, and optimize service delivery.

dm_discussion.para: A customer visits a branch, interacts with staff, and provides feedback about their experience. The feedback ties back to the branch.
A customer provides feedback about an agent at a branch. The branch ties back to the customer via the agent.
A customer interacts with an agent at a branch and provides feedback. The feedback ties back to the customer and branch.
A contract covers a vehicle that generates a claim. Feedback is given about the process, which ties back to the contrac
A customer enters into a contract, insures their vehicle, and files a claim. The claim ties back to the customer.
A claim is tied to a vehicle under a contract held by a customer. The loop completes with another claim by the same customer.
Feedback provided by a customer relates to their contract and payment, creating a loop of interconnected feedback and transactions.
A vehicle is covered under a contract managed by an agent at a specific branch, creating a loop that ties the vehicle back to its branch.

popis_dotazu.para: List of customers, who were examined by Agent Juan Roberts.

dotaz.relacni_algebra: mycustomers[agent_id, first_name, last_name, address] * agents(name = 'Juan Roberts')[agent_id]
dotaz.sql: SELECT mycustomers.first_name, mycustomers.last_name, mycustomers.address, mycustomers.agent_id FROM mycustomers JOIN agents ON mycustomers.agent_id = agents.agent_id WHERE agents.name = 'Juan Roberts';

popis_dotazu.para: List of customers - name, address who were serviced by agent Juan Roberts ONLY (the customers didn't have visits to anothe agent).

dotaz.sql: SELECT DISTINCT mycustomers.first_name, mycustomers.last_name, mycustomers.address, mycustomers.agent_id FROM mycustomers WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM agents WHERE agents.agent_id = mycustomers.agent_id AND agents.name = 'Juan Roberts' ) AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM agents WHERE agents.agent_id = mycustomers.agent_id AND agents.name != 'Juan Roberts' );

popis_dotazu.para: List of customer names and addresses who were associated with agents.

dotaz.sql: SELECT DISTINCT mycustomers.first_name, mycustomers.last_name, mycustomers.address FROM mycustomers JOIN agents ON mycustomers.agent_id = agents.agent_id;

popis_dotazu.para: List of customer names and addresses who have contracts with vehicles of the make 'Toyota'.

dotaz.sql: SELECT DISTINCT mycustomers.first_name, mycustomers.last_name, mycustomers.address FROM vehicles JOIN contracts ON vehicles.contract_id = contracts.contract_id JOIN mycustomers ON contracts.cus_id = mycustomers.cus_id WHERE vehicles.make = 'Toyota';

popis_dotazu.para: Count of coverage types, count of different contract numbers, and sum of contract durations in years.

dotaz.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT contracts.coverage_type) AS count_coverage_types, COUNT(DISTINCT contracts.contract_number) AS count_different_contract_numbers, SUM(DATE_PART('year', contracts.end_date::DATE) - DATE_PART('year', contracts.startdate::DATE)) AS sum_contract_durations FROM contracts;

popis_dotazu.para: Which customers are associated with contracts, and which contracts have no associated customers?

dotaz.sql: SELECT DISTINCT mycustomers.cus_id, mycustomers.first_name, mycustomers.last_name, contracts.contract_id, contracts.contract_number FROM mycustomers FULL OUTER JOIN contracts ON mycustomers.cus_id = contracts.cus_id ORDER BY mycustomers.cus_id, contracts.contract_id;

popis_dotazu.para: List of customers who do not have contracts ending on '2023-12-31'.

dotaz.relacni_algebra: {mycustomers[cus_id,agent_id, first_name, last_name, address,date_of_birth,email]} \ {contracts(end_date='2023-12-31') * mycustomers}[cus_id,agent_id, first_name, last_name, address,date_of_birth,email]
dotaz.sql: SELECT * FROM mycustomers WHERE cus_id NOT IN ( SELECT DISTINCT cus_id FROM contracts WHERE end_date = '2023-12-31' );

popis_dotazu.para: Customers who have contracts for each vehicle of make 'Toyota' produced after 2010.

dotaz.sql: SELECT mycustomers.first_name, mycustomers.last_name, sub.vehicle_id FROM mycustomers JOIN ( SELECT contracts.cus_id, vehicles.vehicle_id FROM contracts JOIN vehicles ON contracts.contract_id = vehicles.contract_id WHERE vehicles.make = 'Toyota' AND vehicles.year > '2010' ) AS sub ON mycustomers.cus_id = sub.cus_id;

popis_dotazu.para: Customers list. For each of them, find the count of contracts they are associated with.

dotaz.sql: SELECT mycustomers.cus_id, mycustomers.first_name, mycustomers.last_name, mycustomers.address, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM contracts WHERE contracts.cus_id = mycustomers.cus_id) AS contract_count FROM mycustomers;

popis_dotazu.para: List of vehicle makes that are associated with contracts.

dotaz.sql: SELECT DISTINCT vehicles.make FROM vehicles WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM contracts WHERE contracts.contract_id = vehicles.contract_id );

popis_dotazu.para: List of contracts (all attributes) that are either associated with a vehicle or have a payment recorded.

dotaz.sql: SELECT contracts.* FROM contracts JOIN vehicles ON contracts.contract_id = vehicles.contract_id UNION SELECT contracts.* FROM contracts JOIN payment ON contracts.contract_id = payment.contract_id;

popis_dotazu.para: List of customers who have not provided feedback.

dotaz.sql: SELECT * FROM mycustomers EXCEPT SELECT mycustomers.* FROM mycustomers JOIN feedback ON mycustomers.cus_id = feedback.cus_id;

popis_dotazu.para: List of contracts (all attributes) that are both associated with vehicles and have payments recorded.

dotaz.sql: SELECT contracts.* FROM contracts JOIN vehicles ON contracts.contract_id = vehicles.contract_id INTERSECT SELECT contracts.* FROM contracts JOIN payment ON contracts.contract_id = payment.contract_id;

popis_dotazu.para: Count of contract types, count of unique customers, and total payment amounts for all contracts.

dotaz.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT contracts.coverage_type) AS count_contract_types, COUNT(DISTINCT contracts.cus_id) AS count_unique_customers, SUM(payment.amount::NUMERIC) AS total_payment_amount FROM contracts LEFT JOIN payment ON contracts.contract_id = payment.contract_id;

popis_dotazu.para: For each customer, find the count of their contracts and total payment amounts

dotaz.sql: SELECT mycustomers.cus_id, mycustomers.first_name, mycustomers.last_name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM contracts WHERE contracts.cus_id = mycustomers.cus_id) AS contract_count, (SELECT SUM(payment.amount::NUMERIC) FROM contracts JOIN payment ON contracts.contract_id = payment.contract_id WHERE contracts.cus_id = mycustomers.cus_id) AS total_payment FROM mycustomers;

popis_dotazu.para: Names of customers younger than 40, who have at least 3 contracts for the coverage type 'Full Coverage'. List is ordered alphabetically by customer name.

dotaz.sql: SELECT mycustomers.cus_id, mycustomers.first_name, mycustomers.last_name FROM mycustomers JOIN contracts ON mycustomers.cus_id = contracts.cus_id WHERE contracts.coverage_type = 'Full Coverage' AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM AGE(mycustomers.date_of_birth)) < 40 GROUP BY mycustomers.cus_id, mycustomers.first_name, mycustomers.last_name HAVING COUNT(contracts.contract_id) >= 3 ORDER BY mycustomers.first_name, mycustomers.last_name;

popis_dotazu.para: For each customer, find the count of their contracts and total payment amounts (if any).using GROUP BY

dotaz.sql: SELECT mycustomers.cus_id, mycustomers.first_name, mycustomers.last_name, COUNT(contracts.contract_id) AS contract_count, SUM(payment.amount::NUMERIC) AS total_payment FROM mycustomers LEFT JOIN contracts ON mycustomers.cus_id = contracts.cus_id LEFT JOIN payment ON contracts.contract_id = payment.contract_id GROUP BY mycustomers.cus_id, mycustomers.first_name, mycustomers.last_name HAVING COUNT(contracts.contract_id) > 0;

popis_dotazu.para: Create a view with contract details, including customer name, branch location, and coverage type.

dotaz.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_contract_details AS SELECT contracts.contract_id, contracts.contract_number, mycustomers.first_name AS customer_first_name, mycustomers.last_name AS customer_last_name, branches.location AS branch_location, contracts.coverage_type, contracts.startdate, contracts.end_date FROM contracts JOIN mycustomers ON contracts.cus_id = mycustomers.cus_id JOIN branches ON contracts.branch_id = branches.branch_id ORDER BY contracts.contract_id;
-- Example usage: Select all contracts that are active as of today. SELECT customer_first_name, customer_last_name, branch_location, coverage_type, startdate, end_date FROM v_contract_details WHERE CURRENT_DATE BETWEEN startdate::DATE AND end_date::DATE ORDER BY startdate;

popis_dotazu.para: Create a view with contract and payment details including customer name, contract number, and payment information.

dotaz.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_contract_payments AS SELECT contracts.contract_id, contracts.contract_number, mycustomers.first_name AS customer_first_name, mycustomers.last_name AS customer_last_name, payment.payment_date, payment.payment_method, payment.amount FROM contracts JOIN mycustomers ON contracts.cus_id = mycustomers.cus_id LEFT JOIN payment ON contracts.contract_id = payment.contract_id ORDER BY contracts.contract_id;
-- Query to retrieve contracts with payments made in the last 30 days. SELECT customer_first_name, customer_last_name, contract_number, payment_date, payment_method, amount FROM v_contract_payments WHERE payment_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' ORDER BY payment_date DESC;

popis_dotazu.para: List of agents (name, email) who were visited by EACH customer who also visited agent Juan Roberts.

dotaz.sql: SELECT agents.name, agents.email FROM agents WHERE NOT EXISTS ( SELECT mycustomers.cus_id FROM mycustomers WHERE mycustomers.cus_id IN ( SELECT mycustomers.cus_id FROM mycustomers JOIN agents ON mycustomers.agent_id = agents.agent_id WHERE agents.name = 'Juan Roberts' ) AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT mycustomers.agent_id FROM mycustomers WHERE mycustomers.cus_id = mycustomers.cus_id AND mycustomers.agent_id = agents.agent_id ) );

popis_dotazu.para: Customers (cus_id, name), paired with vehicles of make 'Toyota'.

dotaz.sql: SELECT mycustomers.cus_id, CONCAT(mycustomers.first_name, ' ', mycustomers.last_name) AS name, vehicles.vehicle_id, vehicles.make, vehicles.model FROM mycustomers CROSS JOIN vehicles WHERE vehicles.make = 'Toyota';

popis_dotazu.para: List of customers (all attributes), who have no feedback recorded.

dotaz.sql: -- variant 1 SELECT * FROM mycustomers WHERE cus_id NOT IN ( SELECT cus_id FROM feedback ); -- variant 2 SELECT * FROM mycustomers WHERE NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM feedback WHERE feedback.cus_id = mycustomers.cus_id ); -- variant 3 SELECT mycustomers.* FROM mycustomers LEFT JOIN feedback ON mycustomers.cus_id = feedback.cus_id WHERE feedback.cus_id IS NULL;

popis_dotazu.para: List of contracts (all attributes) associated with vehicles of the make 'Hyundai.'

dotaz.sql: SELECT DISTINCT contracts.* FROM contracts JOIN vehicles ON contracts.contract_id = vehicles.contract_id WHERE vehicles.make = 'Hyundai';

popis_dotazu.para: List of customers (name, address) who were examined by agent Juan Roberts ONLY (they didn't interact with any other agent).

dotaz.sql: SELECT DISTINCT mycustomers.first_name, mycustomers.last_name, mycustomers.address FROM mycustomers WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM agents WHERE agents.agent_id = mycustomers.agent_id AND agents.name = 'Juan Roberts' ) AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM agents WHERE agents.agent_id = mycustomers.agent_id AND agents.name != 'Juan Roberts' );

popis_dotazu.para: List of customers (name, email) who have contracts and provided feedback for the same branch.

dotaz.sql: SELECT DISTINCT mycustomers.first_name, mycustomers.last_name, mycustomers.email FROM mycustomers JOIN contracts ON mycustomers.cus_id = contracts.cus_id JOIN feedback ON contracts.branch_id = feedback.branch_id WHERE contracts.cus_id = feedback.cus_id;

popis_dotazu.para: Insert payments for all contracts of customer 6 using "Credit Card" and customer 7 using "Bank Transfer," with random amounts between 10,000 and 15,000, after deleting any existing payments for their contracts.
Update the coverage type to 'Premium Coverage' for contracts that have more than 2 associated vehicles.

dotaz.sql: UPDATE contracts SET coverage_type = 'Premium Coverage' WHERE contract_id IN ( SELECT contract_id FROM vehicles GROUP BY contract_id HAVING COUNT(vehicle_id) > 2 );

popis_dotazu.para: Delete contracts that are associated only with vehicles but have no payments or claims.

dotaz.sql: DELETE FROM contracts WHERE contract_id IN ( SELECT contract_id FROM contracts WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM vehicles WHERE vehicles.contract_id = contracts.contract_id ) AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM payment WHERE payment.contract_id = contracts.contract_id ) AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM claims WHERE claims.vehicle_id IN ( SELECT vehicle_id FROM vehicles WHERE vehicles.contract_id = contracts.contract_id ) ) );

popis_dotazu.para: List all customers (with all their attributes) who are not associated with the agent named Monica Walker.

dotaz.sql: SELECT mycustomers.* FROM mycustomers RIGHT JOIN agents ON mycustomers.agent_id = agents.agent_id WHERE agents.name = 'Monica Walker';

popis_dotazu.para: List of customers (all attributes), who have either provided feedback or have an active contract.

dotaz.sql: SELECT DISTINCT mycustomers.* FROM mycustomers JOIN feedback ON mycustomers.cus_id = feedback.cus_id UNION SELECT DISTINCT mycustomers.* FROM mycustomers JOIN contracts ON mycustomers.cus_id = contracts.cus_id;

popis_dotazu.para: Check the validity of agents who were visited by all customers who also visited Juan Roberts. If the result for agent "123 Josef Pavlicek" was returned in D1, the validity check for Pavlicek would ensure that there are no discrepancies in the results.

dotaz.sql: SELECT agents.name, agents.email FROM agents WHERE NOT EXISTS ( SELECT mycustomers.cus_id FROM mycustomers WHERE mycustomers.cus_id IN ( SELECT mycustomers.cus_id FROM mycustomers JOIN agents ON mycustomers.agent_id = agents.agent_id WHERE agents.name = 'Juan Roberts' ) AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT mycustomers.agent_id FROM mycustomers WHERE mycustomers.cus_id = mycustomers.cus_id AND mycustomers.agent_id = agents.agent_id ) ) AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT agents.agent_id FROM agents WHERE NOT EXISTS ( SELECT mycustomers.cus_id FROM mycustomers WHERE mycustomers.agent_id = agents.agent_id AND mycustomers.cus_id NOT IN ( SELECT mycustomers.cus_id FROM mycustomers WHERE mycustomers.agent_id = agents.agent_id ) ) );

popis_dotazu.para: list of customers (with their first and last names) who reserved at least one vehicle of the make 'Toyota'.

dotaz.sql: SELECT DISTINCT mycustomers.first_name, mycustomers.last_name FROM mycustomers JOIN contracts USING(cus_id) JOIN vehicles USING(contract_id) WHERE vehicles.make = 'Toyota';

radek.kategorie_popis: A - Positive query on at least two joined tables
A (RA) - Positive query on at least two joined tables
B - Negative query on at least two joined tables
C - Select only those related to...
C (RA) - Select only those related to...
D1 - Select all related to - universal quantification query
D2 - Result check of D1 query
F1 - JOIN ON
F1 (RA) - JOIN ON
F2 - NATURAL JOIN|JOIN USING
F3 - CROSS JOIN
F4 - LEFT|RIGHT OUTER JOIN
F4 (RA) - LEFT|RIGHT OUTER JOIN
F5 - FULL (OUTER) JOIN
G1 - Nested query in WHERE clause
G1 (RA) - Nested query in WHERE clause
G2 - Nested query in FROM clause
G3 - Nested query in SELECT clause
G4 - Correlated nested query (EXISTS|NOT EXISTS)
G4 (RA) - Correlated nested query (EXISTS|NOT EXISTS)
H1 - Set unification - UNION
H1 (RA) - Set unification - UNION
H2 - Set difference - MINUS or EXCEPT
H3 - Set intersection - INTERSECT
I1 - Aggregate functions (count|sum|min|max|avg)
I2 - Aggregate function over grouped rows - GROUP BY (HAVING)
J - Same query in 3 different sql statements
K - All clauses in one query - SELECT FROM WHERE GROUP BY HAVING ORDER BY
L - View
M - Query over a view
N - INSERT, which insert a set of rows, which are the result of another subquery (an INSERT command which has VALUES clause replaced by a nested query.
O - UPDATE with nested SELECT statement
P - DELETE with nested SELECT statement

conclussions.title: Conclusion
conclussions.para: ### **This project demonstrated the effective application of advanced SQL queries and database management techniques to address diverse real-world scenarios. By utilizing a well-structured schema and realistic data, it explored various query categories, including subqueries, joins, aggregations, set operations, and view creation. Each task was carefully designed to meet specific business requirements, such as identifying unique customer behaviors, managing contract and payment records, and analyzing relationships between entities. The project successfully showcased the design and optimization of efficient SQL queries for data retrieval, manipulation, and transformation while ensuring data integrity through appropriate constraints, joins, and validation techniques. Complex requirements were addressed using advanced SQL constructs, including subqueries, cross joins, unions, and set operations, to extract meaningful insights. Additionally, the creation of reusable and modular queries, such as views and common table expressions (CTEs), demonstrated scalability and maintainability for future analytical tasks. This project highlights the significance of SQL and Relational Algebra as a powerful tools for database management and decision-making, providing a robust foundation for extending these methods to other domains and fostering a deeper understanding of relational databases and their practical applications.**

references.title: References
references.para: [3] Jiří Hunka Youtube Channel (How To Create Sql and RA Queries).
[4] Boat Livery - Sample of Semstral Work
